data.title: Des Moines' Federal Home Loan Bank releases Q3 2025 financials, declares dividend.
data.description: In a recent report, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate has risen by 2% to 6.5% in the last quarter. This increase in unemployment has been attributed to the closure of several businesses during the pandemic, leading to over 300,000 job losses. Additionally, the report highlighted that the average income has decreased by 8% to $45,000, further impacting individuals and families. Experts are now calling for government intervention to address the rising unemployment and income inequality in the country.

data.title: Hot Wheels expands Formula 1 collection to all 10 teams, including Ferrari and Aston Martin, reflecting 2025 liveries.
data.description: In El Segundo, California, Mattel, Inc. (NASDAQ: MAT) has disclosed an updated Hot Wheels Formula 1® collection as part of a multi-year licensing deal with Formula 1®. The collection will now encompass all 10 Formula 1® teams, such as Scuderia Ferrari HP Team and Aston Martin Aramco Formula One™ Team™. These models will feature accurate liveries and driver lineups for the official Formula 1® 2025 season. This expanded lineup will be accessible in both the Hot Wheels core.

data.title: Casago partners with Ximplifi for franchisee trust accounting services, offering scalable financial operations for growth.
data.description: A leading vacation rental property management company, Casago, based in Scottsdale, Arizona, has partnered with Ximplifi, designating them as the preferred provider of accounting and trust accounting services for franchisees. This collaboration offers Casago's franchise partners standardized, compliant, and scalable financial operations. This move aims to empower franchisees within Casago's network by allowing them to concentrate on growth and improving guest experience. This collaboration signifies a strategic move by Casago to enhance its franchise-first model and further support its network's financial management.

data.title: Novanta (Nasdaq: NOVT) to host ISO event in Barcelona regarding humanoid robot safety standards from Oct 27-30, 2025.
data.description: Novanta Inc. (Nasdaq: NOVT), a technology partner for medical and industrial OEMs, will hold an ISO working group event in Barcelona, Spain from October 27-30, 2025, to establish safety standards for humanoid robots. This global initiative seeks to create guidelines for the design and deployment of humanoid robots. The event will convene experts from around the world to develop a safety framework for these robots.

data.title: LambdaTest introduces AI-Powered Web Scanner for visual bugs & accessibility testing, aiding teams at scale.
data.description: In San Francisco, LambdaTest has introduced an AI-Powered Web Scanner that enables teams to identify visual bugs and guarantee accessibility compliance on a large scale. This tool utilizes artificial intelligence to enhance the efficiency of bug detection processes.

data.title: Symetra named to 2025 Fortune Best Workplaces for Women List, recognized by Fortune and Great Place to Work.
data.description: Symetra, based in Bellevue, Washington, has been honored by Fortune magazine and Great Place to Work for making it onto the 2025 Fortune Best Workplaces for Women list.

data.title: Izotropic Corp (CSE: IZO) uses AI in breast CT imaging system. Editorial by NNW highlights placement.
data.description: On October 24, 2025, Izotropic Corporation (CSE: IZO) (OTCQB: IZOZF) disclosed its feature in an editorial released by NetworkNewsWire ("NNW"), a segment of the Dynamic Brand Portfolio @ IBN, a specialized platform highlighting financial news for private and public firms and investors. NNW, one of over 70 brands under IBN, showcased Izotropic Corporation, emphasizing its presence in the market.

data.title: Intel returns to profitability, reports Q3 revenue of $13.7 billion above consensus. Analysts raised price targets: $25, $30, $50, and $34.
data.description: Intel Corp's shares (NASDAQ: INTC) rose after the company's positive third-quarter results were reported. Analysts had mixed ratings on the stock, with Rosenblatt Securities' Kevin Cassidy reiterating a Sell rating but increasing the price target from $14 to $25. Wedbush's Matt Bryson maintained a Neutral rating with a price target of $30, while Benchmark's Cody Acree reaffirmed a Buy rating and raised the price target from $43 to $50. BofA Securities' Vivek Arya kept an Underperform rating with a price target of $34, and Needham's Quinn Bolton stayed with a Hold rating. According to Cassidy, Intel showed "solid progress," with revenues of $13.7 billion surpassing the consensus of $13.2 billion. He mentioned improvements in the balance sheet, rising CPU demand, and cost reductions. Wedbush noted strong PC and server CPU demand, with AI adoption driving growth in cloud and edge computing markets. Management's fourth-quarter revenue guidance of $13.3 billion slightly exceeded consensus estimates. Intel's stock had varying ratings from analysts, emphasizing different aspects of the company's performance in the third quarter.

data.title: YorLabs' Zero-CapEx Intracardiac Imaging System gets FDA 510(k) clearance; major breakthrough in EP and IC procedures.
data.description: YorLabs, Inc., based in Beaverton, Oregon, shared news on Oct. 24, 2025, about their innovative intracardiac imaging solutions for EP and IC procedures. The company, a leader in medical technology, revealed that the FDA has approved their new products. This signifies a significant milestone for YorLabs, as their cutting-edge solutions can now be marketed and utilized in the United States. This development is expected to benefit many patients and healthcare providers in need of advanced EP and IC technologies.

data.title: Polymarket to launch token, airdrop post return to US per CMO Matthew Modabber.
data.description: Polymarket's Chief Marketing Officer, Matthew Modabber, has stated that the platform is planning to release a token and conduct an airdrop. However, this will happen only after Polymarket reenters the U.S. market.
